title: Gemora Danger Level (DL) Scale
shortDesc: Definitions for the Gemora 1-5 danger scale used to rate creatures, anomalies, and zones.
fullDesc:
The Gemora Danger Level (DL) is a 1-5 scale used by Morneven Institute to communicate the threat profile of any catalogued entity, zone, or anomaly.

DL-1 — Negligible. Trained personnel may approach without escort.
DL-2 — Cautionary. Standard PPE required; solo approach permitted.
DL-3 — Hostile. Minimum two-operative team, ranged armament authorized.
DL-4 — Lethal. Specialist field team only; containment doctrine in force.
DL-5 — Existential. No approach is sanctioned; observation from secure orbit only.

The DL rating is reviewed quarterly and may be elevated unilaterally by any Director-tier officer. DL is independent of GEC classification — a low-classification entity can still earn a high DL if engaged carelessly.

title: Gemora Entropy Classification (GEC) — Mark II
shortDesc: The operational standard for categorizing anomalies by structural stability and elimination difficulty.
fullDesc:
The GEC system is the operational standard used by the Morneven Institute to categorize anomalies based on their structural stability and the tactical difficulty of their elimination. Each tier maps loosely to legacy SCP Foundation classifications, retained for cross-reference with pre-collapse archives.

— AMORPHOUS (Stable) —
Low energy signature; passive and non-reactive to the environment.
Protocol: Routine Survey. Periodic observation. Elimination only if obstructing expansion paths.
Legacy equivalent: Safe.

— CRYSTALLINE (Reactive) —
Begins forming attack/defense patterns. Shows collective intelligence or predatory instincts.
Protocol: Tactical Analysis. Identify 'Fracture Points' via long-range observation before engagement.
Legacy equivalent: Euclid.

— METAMORPHIC (Adaptive) —
Capable of mutating or shifting energy frequencies when attacked. Highly unpredictable.
Protocol: High-Intensity Suppression. Immediate, massive force application before the entity can adapt.
Legacy equivalent: Keter.

— CATALYST (Symbiotic) —
Possesses unique properties that stabilize or 'consume' energy from other anomalies.
Protocol: Strategic Asset. Deployed to trigger decay in high-threat targets prior to military intervention.
Legacy equivalent: Thaumiel.

— SINGULARITY (Critical) —
Distorts physical laws (gravity/time). High potential for sector-wide collapse.
Protocol: Sector Purge. Approach strictly prohibited. Observation from secure orbit only. Elimination currently impossible.
Legacy equivalent: Apollyon.

— ZERO-STATE (Decayed) —
Structure completely destroyed or permanently stripped of anomalous properties.
Protocol: Confirmed Elimination. File closed. Residual materials processed for research or discarded.
Legacy equivalent: Neutralized.

title: The Morneven Doctrine: Elimination Over Containment
shortDesc: Why the Institute rejects 'Secure, Contain, Protect' in favor of Observation, Neutralization, and Elimination.
fullDesc:
Unlike pre-apocalyptic frameworks that focused on 'Secure, Contain, Protect,' the Morneven Institute operates on a philosophy of Observation, Neutralization, and Elimination. This shift is driven by four core principles.

— A. The Law of Crystalline Entropy —
In the Gemora system, anomalies act like a spreading crystal growth. Containing them in a single facility creates a 'pressure cooker' effect — concentrating anomalous energy until it inevitably mutates or breaches. For Morneven, containment is merely a delayed catastrophe.

— B. Deep Space Resource Efficiency —
Maintaining high-security containment sites across the void is logistically and energetically unsustainable. It is far more cost-effective to utilize those resources for permanent neutralization, ensuring that interstellar navigation routes remain 'clean' and predictable.

— C. Post-Apocalyptic Pragmatism —
The world has already suffered a total collapse due to the failures of old-world systems. Morneven believes that human survival depends on the removal of uncontrollable variables. If an entity is anomalous, it is a 'flaw' in the structure of reality that must be corrected through removal.

— D. Observation for Optimization —
Morneven does not observe to 'understand' the life of an anomaly, but to find its Fracture Point. Every second of study is dedicated to finding the most efficient, low-cost method to shatter the entity's structure. We study the enemy solely to perfect the kill.

title: The Gemosphere
shortDesc: Overview of the crystalline atmospheric layer that defines post-apocalyptic Earth.
fullDesc:
The Gemosphere is the suspended crystalline particulate layer that replaced the lower stratosphere following the Final Resonance. It refracts sunlight into the characteristic violet dawns observed across all settled territories and is the source of the trace minerals that drove most post-event mutation events.

Understanding the Gemosphere is foundational to nearly every Institute research vertical, from terraforming to Jewerlian metaphysics.
